name: Balcón de Madrid and Soto de Viñuelas Loop from Tres Cantos
name_original: Balcón de Madrid – circular desde Tres Cantos por los Caminos del Canal
description: This moderate 26.3-mile mountain biking route from Tres Cantos offers scenic views, aqueducts, and forest roads along the Caminos del Canal.
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Tres Cantos are situated just north of Madrid, offering a diverse natural environment for outdoor activities. The region features varied terrain, from dedicated bike park facilities to extensive trail networks traversing natural spaces like Montes del Pardo and Monte de Viñuelas. Riders encounter Mediterranean ecosystems, arroyos, and areas with significant elevation changes. Best mountain bike trails around Tres Cantos The most popular mountain bike trail is Balcón de Madrid and Soto de Viñuelas Loop from Tres Cantos, a 26.3 miles (42.4 km) trail that takes 3 hours 32 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers views of the surrounding natural parks. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Monte Coto Pesadilla – Viñuelas Stream Bridge loop from Tres Cantos, a moderate 26.1 miles (42.1 km) path. This trail explores the area's streams and natural landscapes. Local mountain bikers also love the Pontón de la Culebra Aqueduct – Dehesa Boyal Fountain loop from Tres Cantos, a 23.9 miles (38.6 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 3 hours 6 minutes. Mountain biking around Tres Cantos is defined by its proximity to Montes del Pardo and Monte de Viñuelas, diverse singletracks, and Mediterranean ecosystems. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy green singletracks to challenging routes. The routes in Tres Cantos are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 450 reviews. More than 2700 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Tres Cantos's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of mountain bike trails can I expect around Tres Cantos?
A: Tres Cantos offers a diverse range of mountain bike trails, from dedicated bike park facilities with various jump lines and circuits to an extensive network of natural trails. You'll find everything from easy green singletracks to more challenging routes that require advanced riding skills, some even involving pushing your bike. The terrain varies, encompassing paved paths, singletracks, and areas with significant elevation changes.

Q: How many mountain bike trails are there in Tres Cantos?
A: There are over 60 mountain bike trails around Tres Cantos recorded on komoot. These include approximately 16 easy routes, 33 moderate routes, and 13 difficult routes, ensuring options for all skill levels.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike trails in Tres Cantos?
A: Yes, Tres Cantos offers several family-friendly options. The local council promotes environmental routes suitable for both cycling and walking, ranging from 5 to 21 km. One easy circular route is "Valdeloshielos," and another connects Valdeloshielos to the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/957557" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Marmot Bridge&lt;/a&gt;, offering a chance to explore Mediterranean biodiversity. Additionally, the Madrid section of the Camino de Santiago passes through Tres Cantos, providing an 11 km family-friendly route along the Arroyo Tejada.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or viewpoints along the mountain bike trails?
A: Many routes incorporate historical and scenic points. You can visit the medieval &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/957557" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Marmot Bridge&lt;/a&gt;, a key attraction on several trails. For panoramic views, consider routes that pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/957559" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valdeliganar Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1656436" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valpalomero Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/868170" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Solana Lookout Tower&lt;/a&gt; also offers interesting sights.

Q: Are there any waterfalls accessible by mountain bike in the area?
A: Yes, you can find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6344971" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hervidero Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; in the natural monuments around Tres Cantos. While not directly on every mountain bike trail, it's a notable natural feature in the region that can be incorporated into longer rides or visited separately.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Tres Cantos?
A: The mountain bike trails around Tres Cantos are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 450 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse terrain, the proximity to natural parks like Montes del Pardo and Monte de Viñuelas, and the variety of routes suitable for different ability levels.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes available?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike routes around Tres Cantos are circular. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e961432560/balcon-de-madrid-and-soto-de-vinuelas-loop-from-tres-cantos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Balcón de Madrid and Soto de Viñuelas Loop from Tres Cantos&lt;/a&gt; is a popular circular route covering over 42 km. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6940855" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monte Coto Pesadilla – Viñuelas Stream Bridge loop from Tres Cantos&lt;/a&gt;, which explores streams and natural landscapes.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Tres Cantos?
A: Tres Cantos, being just north of Madrid, experiences a Mediterranean climate. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant conditions for mountain biking, with milder temperatures and less extreme weather. Summers can be hot, while winters are typically mild but can have cooler, wetter days. Always check the local forecast before heading out.

Q: Is there a dedicated bike park in Tres Cantos?
A: Yes, Tres Cantos features a dedicated Bikepark located on Avenida de los Artesanos. This park offers various jump lines and circuits like P3 Dirt, Mesetas, Dual, Trail Art, and Big Line, catering to riders of all skill levels. Many of its trails are also suitable for e-biking.

Q: Are there any challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. Tres Cantos offers challenging routes for experienced riders, with 13 routes classified as difficult on komoot. A demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10417989" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valdeliganar Viewpoint – Batán Bridge loop from Tres Cantos&lt;/a&gt;, which extends for nearly 93 km and involves significant elevation gains, making it suitable for those seeking a rigorous ride.

Q: Can I access the mountain bike trails using public transport?
A: Tres Cantos is well-connected to Madrid by public transport, including commuter trains (Cercanías) and buses. Many trails are accessible from the town center, making it feasible to reach starting points without a car. It's advisable to check specific route starting points and public transport schedules for the most convenient access.

Q: Where can I park my car when visiting the mountain bike trails?
A: Tres Cantos offers various parking options within the town, especially near residential areas and commercial zones. For trails starting directly from the town, street parking or public car parks are usually available. For routes venturing further into natural areas, look for designated parking spots near trailheads or access points to natural parks like Monte de Viñuelas.
tips:
Geodesic Vertex of Valdelagua, built on August 19, 1976. Located on a small hill of uncultivated land, with some chaparros.
Access: from San Agustín de Guadalix, along the road to Colmenar Viejo, upon reaching Km. 9,600, you enter on the left along a road with an indicator for "C.I. II Canal de El Atazar". At 400 m. it turns to the left, after 4 km it forks; continue to the left, then the road opens in two to join again after 5.3 km, after 5.6 km there is a crossroads, continue straight, after 8 km turn left through the ravine and after 8.2 km you reach the signal, which is about 5 m. on the left.
https://bigjoe.es/ruta-tres-cantos-embalse-de-pedrezuela/
Small hermitage belonging to the town of San Agustín del Guadalix. It is at the end of a small climb inside the Dehesa de Moncalvillo. Good views.
One of the wide views of Madrid. Easy to drive on.

Scenic easy going trail with good surface and width. It runs between fields through the Zarzalejo area to the junction with the railway and allows you to reach the Balcón de Madrid, a viewpoint with very nice views.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: This circular mountain biking route, covering approximately 42.4 kilometers, usually takes around 3.5 hours to complete. This duration is for active riding time and can vary based on your fitness level, speed, and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery or highlights.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Balcón de Madrid – circular desde Tres Cantos por los Caminos del Canal?
faqs.answer: The terrain primarily consists of well-maintained tracks and forest roads, especially along the Caminos del Canal de Isabel II. While the initial kilometers from Tres Cantos are urban, the route quickly transitions into more tranquil sections. You'll encounter impressive structures like aqueducts and even viaducts of the AVE high-speed train, offering a varied and interesting ride.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for mountain biking beginners?
faqs.answer: This route is rated as moderate and requires a good physical condition. While it's generally not difficult to navigate thanks to continuous signage, its length (42.4 km) and elevation gain (575 meters) mean it's best suited for riders with some mountain biking experience rather than absolute beginners.

faqs.question: What are the best times of year to ride this route?
faqs.answer: Spring is particularly recommended for this route, as the scenery is exceptionally green and pleasant. Autumn also offers beautiful colors and comfortable temperatures. While possible year-round, summer can be very hot, and winter might bring colder, wetter conditions.

faqs.question: Where can I park and access the trailhead for this route?
faqs.answer: The route starts and ends in Tres Cantos. You can find parking in the urban areas of Tres Cantos, which serves as a convenient starting point for this circular trail. Specific parking details may vary, so it's advisable to check local parking regulations upon arrival.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the Balcón de Madrid – circular desde Tres Cantos por los Caminos del Canal?
faqs.answer: While the trail passes through natural areas, including the ZEPA Soto de Viñuelas, specific regulations for dogs can vary. It's generally recommended to keep dogs on a leash to protect local wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all users. Always check local signage for any specific restrictions.

faqs.question: What interesting landmarks or viewpoints can I see along the way?
faqs.answer: The route offers several notable points of interest. You'll pass by the Valdelagua Geodetic Vertex with Views of the Sierra de Guadarrama, the historic Hermitage of the Virgin of Navalazarza, and the scenic Madrid Balcony. The trail also integrates with the infrastructure of the Canal de Isabel II, featuring impressive aqueducts and viaducts.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other well-known trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route utilizes and intersects with several official trails. You'll find sections that overlap with the Vereda de las Tapias de del Monte de Viñuelas, Camino de la Moraleja, Cañada del Arroyo de las Caceras, Camino Bajo de Pedrezuela, and Cañada de Vinatea, among others. This integration offers a diverse riding experience through established paths.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required to access this trail or the ZEPA Soto de Viñuelas park?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to ride this trail or to access the ZEPA Soto de Viñuelas. However, as it is a protected natural area, it's crucial to respect the environment, stay on marked paths, and follow any local regulations to preserve its high environmental value.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or restaurants near the trail for refreshments?
faqs.answer: Since the route starts and ends in Tres Cantos, you'll find various amenities, including cafes and restaurants, within the town before or after your ride. Along the more natural sections of the trail, options for refreshments are limited, so it's advisable to carry enough water and snacks.
